competency_groups.title: Electric Charges and Fields
competency_groups.description: Understanding electric charges, conservation of charge, Coulomb's law, superposition principle, continuous charge distribution, electric field, electric field lines, electric dipole, torque on a dipole, electric flux, and Gauss's theorem applications.
competencies: Apply Coulomb's law to calculate force between point charges
Use superposition principle for forces between multiple charges
Analyze continuous charge distribution scenarios
Describe electric field due to point charge and dipole
Interpret electric field lines and flux
Apply Gauss's theorem to find fields of symmetric charge distributions

competency_groups.title: Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance
competency_groups.description: Mastering concepts of electric potential, potential difference, equipotential surfaces, electrical potential energy, conductors, dielectrics, capacitors, capacitance, and energy storage.
competencies: Calculate electric potential and potential difference
Determine electrical potential energy of charge systems
Analyze behavior of conductors and dielectrics
Compute capacitance for various configurations
Evaluate energy stored in capacitors

competency_groups.title: Current Electricity
competency_groups.description: Exploring electric current, drift velocity, Ohm's law, resistivity, internal resistance, Kirchhoff's rules, and Wheatstone bridge.
competencies: Relate drift velocity to electric current
Apply Ohm's law to linear and non-linear circuits
Calculate resistivity and conductivity
Analyze temperature dependence of resistance
Use Kirchhoff's rules for circuit analysis
Operate Wheatstone bridge for resistance measurement

competency_groups.title: Moving Charges and Magnetism
competency_groups.description: Investigating magnetic fields, Biot-Savart law, Ampere's law, forces on moving charges and current-carrying conductors, torque on current loops, and galvanometers.
competencies: Apply Biot-Savart law to calculate magnetic fields
Use Ampere's law for symmetric current distributions
Calculate force on moving charges in magnetic fields
Determine force between parallel current-carrying conductors
Analyze torque on current loops and magnetic dipoles

competency_groups.title: Magnetism and Matter
competency_groups.description: Understanding magnetic properties of materials, magnetic dipoles, magnetization, and temperature effects on magnetic properties.
competencies: Classify materials as para-, dia-, or ferro-magnetic
Analyze magnetic field intensity due to dipoles
Evaluate torque on magnetic dipoles in uniform fields
Explain magnetization and temperature effects

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Induction
competency_groups.description: Studying electromagnetic induction, Faraday's laws, Lenz's law, self and mutual induction.
competencies: Apply Faraday's laws to calculate induced EMF
Use Lenz's law to determine direction of induced current
Analyze self and mutual induction phenomena

competency_groups.title: Alternating Current
competency_groups.description: Exploring AC characteristics, reactance, impedance, LCR circuits, resonance, power factor, and transformers.
competencies: Calculate peak and RMS values of AC quantities
Analyze reactance and impedance in AC circuits
Study resonance in LCR series circuits
Evaluate power factor and wattless current

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Waves
competency_groups.description: Understanding displacement current, electromagnetic waves characteristics, transverse nature, and the electromagnetic spectrum.
competencies: Explain the concept of displacement current
Describe characteristics and transverse nature of EM waves
Identify regions of the electromagnetic spectrum

competency_groups.title: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments
competency_groups.description: Mastering reflection, refraction, spherical mirrors, lenses, optical instruments, and their magnifying powers.
competencies: Apply mirror and lens formulas to image formation
Analyze total internal reflection and optical fibers
Calculate magnification and power of optical instruments
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments

competency_groups.title: Wave Optics
competency_groups.description: Studying wave fronts, Huygen's principle, interference, diffraction, and coherence.
competencies: Apply Huygen's principle to reflection and refraction
Analyze interference patterns and fringe width
Explain diffraction phenomena qualitatively

competency_groups.title: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
competency_groups.description: Exploring photoelectric effect, particle nature of light, matter waves, and de-Broglie relation.
competencies: Explain photoelectric effect using Einstein's equation
Apply de-Broglie relation to matter waves
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

competency_groups.title: Atoms
competency_groups.description: Understanding atomic models, Bohr model, hydrogen spectra, and electron orbits.
competencies: Describe Rutherford's and Bohr's atomic models
Calculate radius, velocity, and energy of electron orbits

competency_groups.title: Nuclei
competency_groups.description: Studying nuclear composition, binding energy, mass-energy relation, fission, and fusion.
competencies: Explain nuclear force and binding energy per nucleon
Analyze nuclear fission and fusion processes

competency_groups.title: Electronic Devices
competency_groups.description: Understanding energy bands, semiconductors, p-n junctions, diodes, and their applications.
competencies: Classify materials based on energy bands
Analyze I-V characteristics of p-n junction diodes
Apply diodes as rectifiers in circuits
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
